name: Poisson a La Provencale
recipeIngredient: 1 can Peeled tomatoes with most juice poured off (28 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely chop the onions, garlic, and 7 ounces of mushrooms.
2. Brown the chopped onions in oil over medium heat until softened.
3. Add the minced garlic and sauté for an additional minute.
4. Add the remaining mushrooms to the pan and sauté until lightly browned.
5. Stir in the tomatoes, fennel, bay leaf, salt, and pepper.
6. Simmer the sauce gently for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Remove from heat and pass through a sieve or food mill. Discard solids.
8. Add a dash of Tabasco sauce to the sauce; it can be made ahead and refrigerated.
9. Slice the remaining mushrooms and sauté in approximately 2 tablespoons butter for 3-5 minutes until lightly browned.
10. In a large sauté pan, brown the fish fillets in butter over medium heat until golden brown on both sides.
11. Carefully pour the warm cognac over the fish and ignite with a flame from a long stick or torch.
12. Transfer the fish to a baking dish, pour the sauce over it, and garnish with sautéed mushrooms.
13. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 15 minutes.
14. Serve hot with rice.
